title: Shirou Emiya
content:
Shirou Emiya is the protagonist of Fate/stay night and the Master of Saber in the Fifth Holy Grail War. He is the adopted son of Kiritsugu Emiya, the Magus Killer who participated in the Fourth Holy Grail War (Fate/Zero).

Shirou survived the Fuyuki City fire — the disaster caused by the Fourth Holy Grail War's conclusion — as the sole survivor. Kiritsugu found him and adopted him, implanting his own Origin Bullet-damaged body's wish into the boy along with the Noble Phantasm Avalon.

As a magus, Shirou is exceptionally poorly trained and his magical circuits are few and of low quality, having been damaged by his near-death experience. However, he has an innate talent for Reinforcement and Projection — two applications of magecraft that allow him to analyze the structure of objects and project copies of them.

Shirou's magical Origin and Alignment are 'Sword,' meaning his entire existence is oriented around swords as a concept. This makes his Projection of swords extraordinarily powerful compared to what his magus rank would suggest. Over the course of Fate/stay night's three routes, he gradually develops this ability.

In the Fate route, Shirou and Saber develop a close relationship as he helps her confront her guilt over her kingship. He fights with reckless disregard for his own life, a trait rooted in the survivor's guilt from the Fuyuki fire.

In the Unlimited Blade Works route, Shirou's confrontation with Archer — his future self — forces him to examine and reaffirm his ideal of being a 'Hero of Justice' even knowing the cost. He unlocks Unlimited Blade Works in this route.

In the Heaven's Feel route, Shirou makes the choice to prioritize saving Sakura over the world, ultimately sacrificing his ability to use magecraft and nearly his life to stop the Greater Grail.

title: Kiritsugu Emiya
content:
Kiritsugu Emiya, known as the Magus Killer, is the Master of Saber (Artoria Pendragon) in the Fourth Holy Grail War as depicted in Fate/Zero. He is a freelance magus-killer and assassin who works for the Einzbern family and later becomes the adoptive father of Shirou Emiya.

Kiritsugu was trained by his adoptive mother, Natalia Kaminski, as a magus hunter. He uses a combination of mundane firearms, magical enhancements, and a unique mystic code to combat magi. His signature technique is the Contraction and Expansion of Time Origin Bullet — bullets made from his own ribs that are imbued with his Origin (Severing and Binding), which destroys a magus's Magic Circuits when shot, permanently crippling their ability to use magecraft.

Kiritsugu's personal philosophy is ruthless utilitarianism: he believes in sacrificing the few to save the many, and will kill innocent people if necessary to prevent greater casualties. This ideology puts him in direct conflict with Saber, who adheres to ideals of chivalry and believes means matter as much as ends.

He seeks the Holy Grail because he believes it can grant his wish: world peace through eliminating all conflict. His tragic arc in Fate/Zero reveals that the Grail is corrupted and will not grant wishes as intended — when he commands it to destroy itself, his wish is twisted into the Fuyuki fire.

Kiritsugu's relationship with Irisviel von Einzbern — who serves as Saber's proxy Master — is one of the emotional cores of Fate/Zero. He is deeply in love with her despite knowing she was created to become the vessel for the Holy Grail.

After the Fourth Grail War, Kiritsugu lived in Fuyuki, found Shirou, and spent his remaining years (cursed by the Grail's mud) as a father figure who quietly passed on his dream of being a Hero of Justice — a dream Shirou internalized deeply.
